rules.title: Use core nodes when possible
rules.description: Use ComfyUI core nodes if possible; custom nodes only when no core node exists. Custom nodes rely on third-party updates, risking template breakage. Core nodes are maintainable by Comfy Org and offer better compatibility for local users and stable long-term maintenance.

rules.title: Never use Set/Get nodes
rules.description: Do not use Set and Get nodes in templates. While they can simplify the workflow, they make it hard for users to follow the logic.

rules.title: Build and test on Comfy Cloud
rules.description: Build the workflow on Cloud, then share the workflow link. The workflow will be tested on cloud to verify it works.

rules.title: Thumbnail requirements
rules.description: Use workflow effect previews, not screenshots. Keep style consistent with existing templates. Ratio: 1:1. If it's a video, keep to 3-5s. You can use the input/output. Supported types at: https://github.com/Comfy-Org/workflow_templates?tab=readme-ov-file#4--choose-thumbnail-type

rules.title: Avoid key info in top-left corner
rules.description: Please avoid key information in the top-left corner of thumbnails. Provider info will be added there if the template contains an API node.

rules.title: No identically-named subgraphs with different internals
rules.description: Don't copy subgraphs with the same name and modify parameters internally, as this leads to logical confusion. Move different settings outside the subgraph, making it easier to read and understand.

rules.title: Subgraph content specifications
rules.description: Preview nodes and Save nodes should be placed outside subgraphs. Avoid storing additional content inside subgraphs or including preview nodes with no practical significance. If sections use the same logic, convert to a subgraph. Refer to Core Subgraph Blueprints guideline.

rules.title: Note nodes must use black background
rules.description: Note nodes should use black to distinguish from the yellow color of API nodes, avoiding user confusion.

rules.title: API nodes colored yellow
rules.description: API nodes are colored yellow. Note nodes must be black to avoid confusion with API node coloring.

rules.title: Use default ComfyUI group color
rules.description: Use the default ComfyUI group color to ensure good readability in both light and dark modes.

rules.title: Simplicity and readability
rules.description: Workflow logic should be clear and easy to understand, not limited to a single template scenario, and should have certain universality to facilitate user understanding and reuse.

rules.title: Template naming rules
rules.description: Make sure all required fields are filled out when submitting. Follow the existing naming patterns in the template library.

submission_process: Build the workflow on Comfy Cloud
Test the workflow on cloud to verify it works
Submit via the Notion submission form with all required fields
Provide thumbnail (1:1 ratio, effect preview not screenshot)

thumbnail_requirements.avoid: screenshots, key info in top-left corner
